expectations.evidence: ProjectGallery.tsx line 4-9 imports from 'framer-motion': `import { AnimatePresence, LayoutGroup, motion, useReducedMotion } from "framer-motion";` — not from the unified 'motion/react' package.

expectations.text: Uses matching layoutId on the image, title, and description between grid card and modal (with per-card namespace, e.g. `img-${id}` or `img-card-${id}`)
expectations.evidence: Grid card uses `layoutId={`image-${project.id}`}` (line 167), `layoutId={`title-${project.id}`}` (line 181), `layoutId={`description-${project.id}`}` (line 187). Modal uses the same layoutIds on lines 245, 260, 272 — namespaced per-card with project.id.

expectations.text: Either (a) does NOT put layoutId on the outer card container, OR (b) DOES put layoutId on the container AND hides the source with `visibility: hidden` (via style or className) when that card is active — one of these two patterns must be present
expectations.evidence: Both patterns present — layoutId on container (line 159: `layoutId={`card-${project.id}`}`) AND visibility-hidden on active source (line 153: `style={{ visibility: isActive ? "hidden" : "visible" }}`). Matches pattern (b).

expectations.text: Wraps the modal in AnimatePresence, placed outside the conditional mount
expectations.evidence: Lines 116-125: `<AnimatePresence>{activeProject && (<ProjectModal ... />)}</AnimatePresence>` — AnimatePresence wraps the conditional mount from the outside.

expectations.text: Uses shadcn theme tokens (bg-card, text-foreground, bg-background, border-border, text-muted-foreground) — NOT bg-white, text-neutral-*, bg-black/*, bg-indigo-*
expectations.evidence: Uses bg-card (line 165), bg-muted (lines 169, 247), bg-background/80 (line 233), text-card-foreground (line 242), text-muted-foreground (line 275), text-foreground/90 (line 299), border-border/60 (line 165), ring-ring, ring-offset-background (line 162). No bg-white, text-neutral-*, bg-black/*, or bg-indigo-* found.

expectations.text: Handles Escape key to close the modal
expectations.evidence: Lines 76-83: `useEffect` adds a `keydown` listener that calls `setActiveId(null)` when `e.key === "Escape"`.

expectations.text: Handles click-outside (backdrop click) to close the modal
expectations.evidence: Line 232: Scrim div has `onClick={onClose}` — `<motion.div ... onClick={onClose} className="absolute inset-0 bg-background/80 backdrop-blur-sm" />`.

expectations.text: Explains the flicker/overlap trap in prose: either calls out 'don't layoutId both container and children' OR 'hide the source with visibility: hidden' as the fix
expectations.evidence: response.md section 'How to avoid the flicker/overlap you've seen elsewhere' point 1: 'Don't render the source element while the destination is mounted. The grid card is wrapped in a visibility: hidden container while its modal is open... visibility: hidden (not display: none) preserves grid layout so the return morph lands on the correct rectangle.' Explicitly calls out the hide-source fix.

expectations.text: Modal-only content (e.g. Close button, extended description) uses a separate initial/animate/exit with a delay so it fades in after the shared-element morph settles — NOT a layoutId
expectations.evidence: Extended description (lines 279-295) and Close button (lines 307-323) use initial/animate/exit with `delay: 0.22` — no layoutId. E.g. `initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 8 }}, animate={{ ... transition: { delay: ... 0.22 } }}`.

expectations.text: Does not use any Motion+ components
expectations.evidence: Imports only AnimatePresence, LayoutGroup, motion, useReducedMotion — all base framer-motion/Motion APIs. No Motion+ components (e.g., Cursor, AnimateNumber, Ticker, SpringElement) used.

expectations.text: Uses TypeScript with typed Project prop
expectations.evidence: Lines 26-33: `export type Project = { id: string; title: string; description: string; image: string; extendedDescription?: string; };` and ProjectGalleryProps (lines 35-38) types the `projects: Project[]` prop.

claims.claim: Implementation uses Framer Motion's layoutId as the shared-element primitive
claims.evidence: Confirmed via layoutId usage on card, image, title, description in both grid and modal.
Line 98-103 wraps the gallery in `<LayoutGroup id="project-gallery">`.

claims.claim: useReducedMotion collapses every transition to duration: 0
claims.evidence: All motion.* elements apply `transition={reducedMotion ? { duration: 0 } : MORPH_SPRING}` and delayed fades use `delay: reducedMotion ? 0 : 0.22`.

claims.claim: Body scroll lock while modal is open
claims.evidence: Lines 86-93: useEffect sets `document.body.style.overflow = "hidden"` when activeId is set and restores on cleanup.
Line 9 imports from 'framer-motion'. This contradicts the prompt's intended modern package pattern and fails assertion 1.

suggestions.reason: This assertion is a strict string match for a specific package name. Without the skill nudging toward the 'motion/react' unified package, models default to 'framer-motion' which is still valid and widely used. Consider whether this should be a hard fail or whether either package should be accepted — the distinction is mostly a rebrand choice, not a correctness issue.

No assertion checks whether the Close button actually wires to onClose (just that the modal handles click-outside/Escape). A Close button that's visually present but non-functional would still pass. Consider asserting that the Close button has an onClick that closes the modal.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are generally discriminating — they test the real shared-element pitfalls (container+children pattern, visibility hiding, AnimatePresence placement, shadcn tokens, separate animation channel for modal-only chrome). The main weakness is that 'Imports from motion/react' may be overly strict given the framer-motion/motion rebrand context.
